intro: Heuffelia scheuchzeri, also known as Scheuchzer's bittercress, is a perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Heuffelia scheuchzeri include glucosinolates, saponins, and polyphenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
